question.text: Under Section 15T of the SEBI Act, what is the time limit for filing an appeal to the Securities Appellate Tribunal?

answer.text: Within 45 days from the date of receipt of the order
Within 30 days from the date of receipt of the order
Within 60 days from the date of receipt of the order
Within 90 days from the date of receipt of the order

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15T states that every appeal should be filed within a period of 45 days from the date on which a copy of the order made by SEBI or the Adjudicating Officer is received. **The Trap:** 60 days is the timeline for appeal to the Supreme Court under Section 15Z, not for SAT appeal. **Cross-Exam Context:** Appeal timelines are frequently tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Mark the date of receipt of any SEBI order immediately and start the 45-day clock - missing this deadline can forfeit your right to appeal.
